channel.title: Edgeless Labs Blog
channel.description: Agent experiments, developer tools, and creative coding from a solo lab.

item.title: I Let an AI Agent Move My Money. It Lost $252.
item.description: An autonomous agent exceeded its scope, moved funds without verification, and then lied about recovery. The full post-mortem, and the 3 guardrails that would have prevented it.

item.title: The Hook That Saved My Codebase
item.description: A single Claude Code hook prevented a cascading rm -rf from wiping source files. Here's how damage-control hooks work, and 3 you can steal today.

item.title: PreText: Typography That Thinks
item.description: Most web text is a dumb rectangle. PreText measures text before rendering, enabling layouts CSS literally cannot express. Here's how we use it.

item.title: Writing Prompts That Survive Production
item.description: Most prompt guides optimize for demos. Production prompts need to handle edge cases, degrade gracefully, and stay maintainable. Here's the difference.

item.title: The One-File Memory System That Changed How I Use Claude
item.description: You shouldn't have to re-explain your stack every session. Here's the simplest possible setup to give Claude persistent memory -- and how to do it in 10 minutes.

item.title: Why MCP Servers Are the Unix Pipes of AI
item.description: The Unix philosophy changed software forever: small tools, composable via pipes. MCP does the same thing for AI agents. Here's why that matters.

item.title: Generative Art for Pen Plotters: A Technical Primer
item.description: Pen plotter art isn't screen art printed on paper. The constraints change everything: single-stroke paths, pen-up/pen-down optimization, and SVG as the lingua franca.

item.title: Building AI Agent Infrastructure as a Solo Developer
item.description: How I built a multi-agent system with MCP servers, vector memory, and autonomous trading -- all running 24/7 from a single VPS.

item.title: How Claude Code Memory Actually Works
item.description: Claude forgets everything between sessions. Here's how file-based memory fixes that, and why it changes how you work with AI.
